subject: Public Health
title: Mortality and Morbidity Rates
description: Learn Mortality and Morbidity Rates in one minute.

hook.text: Mortality counts deaths. Morbidity counts illness. Together they paint a picture of population health.

buildup.text: Crude mortality rate divides total deaths by total population over a set time period.

discovery.text: Age-adjusted rates let you compare countries fairly—a young population naturally has fewer deaths.

twist.text: A disease can have low mortality but high morbidity—like malaria, which sickens millions who survive.

climax.text: Tracking both tells you not just who dies but who suffers—crucial for setting priorities.

punchline.text: Deaths grab headlines. Illness shapes daily life.

quiz.question: What does morbidity measure?
options: The rate of illness in a population
The rate of death in a population
